title: 10 best herbal creams for back pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which effectively soothe and relieve muscle tension. The cream's active ingredients, such as thujone and camphor, work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, providing instant relief for strained muscles and joints. Additionally, wormwood's natural antibacterial and antifungal properties help prevent infection and promote healthy tissue growth, making it an effective treatment option for chronic back pain sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Wormwood cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like camphor, menthol, and thymol, which help reduce inflammation in the muscles and joints, providing relief from back pain.

Pain-relieving terpenes: Terpenes like borneol, bornyl acetate, and caryophyllene found in wormwood cream have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing back pain and discomfort.

Antispasmodic flavonoids: Flavonoids like artemisinin and sesquiterpenes in wormwood cream help relax muscle spasms and reduce muscle tension, which can contribute to back pain, providing relief from cramps, stiffness, and strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots are valued for their bitter compounds, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate back pain.
Barks: Wormwood barks contain flavonoids and terpenes that provide analgesic and antispasmodic effects, making them useful in treating back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried artemisia absinthium leaves and stems from a trusted supplier or your own garden.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax, 30 grams of coconut oil, and 40 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add the powdered artemisia absinthium to the melted mixture and stir continuously for 10 minutes to ensure uniform distribution.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marijuana creams helps with back pain because they contain cannabinoids that interact with the body's endocannabinoid system, providing targeted relief to affected areas. The topical application allows for direct delivery of the active ingredients to the site of pain, bypassing the need for systemic absorption. This localized approach enables the cream to effectively reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with back pain, promoting relaxation and improving overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Cannabidiol (CBD): CBD has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help reduce back pain by decreasing inflammation and relieving pain sensations.

Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC): THC is a natural pain-reliever that binds to cannabinoid receptors in the body, reducing pain perception and promoting relaxation, which can be especially helpful for individuals with chronic back pain.

Terpenes: Terpenes, such as beta-caryophyllene and humulene, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with back pain, while also providing a sense of relief and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in cannabinoids, terpenes, and flavonoids, roots provide a concentrated source of active ingredients for pain relief.
Stems: The woody stems of the marijuana plant contain lignin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce back pain.
remedy_recipe: Decoct dried cannabis sativa leaves in organic coconut oil at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 4 hours and filter the mixture through cheesecloth.
Combine equal parts of decocoted oil and beeswax in a double boiler, heating until the wax melts and stirring occasionally over 20 minutes.
Add a tablespoon of lavender essential oil and two teaspoons of vitamin e oil to the cooled mixture, blending well and stirring for an additional minute.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and store at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate for longer storage.
Apply a thin layer of the herbal cannabis sativa cream topically to affected back areas twice daily as needed, massaging gently until fully absorbed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with back pain because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and improve circulation. The natural compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, work together to ease muscle tension and stiffness, providing relief from aching backs. Additionally, sage's warming properties help increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing pain. This makes herbal sage creams an effective natural remedy for alleviating back pain, offering temporary relief from discomfort and stiffness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviate back pain by soothing irritated tissues and muscles.
Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic back pain and stiffness.
Pain-relieving terpenes: Sage contains terpenes like camphor and bornyl acetate, which have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help relieve back pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Rhyzomes: Contains salicylates, which are similar to aspirin and help reduce pain, swelling, and inflammation caused by back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and dry them in a warm dark place for at least two weeks.
Combine dried salvia officinalis leaves with 250ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a heatproof glass container.
Heat the mixture under low heat for three to five hours until it reaches a temperature of 60-80 degrees celsius.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and essential oils such as peppermint and eucalyptus in a double boiler until it reaches a consistency similar to body lotion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper creams helps with back pain because they contain a unique combination of natural compounds that target the underlying causes of discomfort. The creams' anti-inflammatory properties, derived from bell peppers, work to reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on the affected area, providing relief for strained muscles and joints. Additionally, the creams' soothing botanicals help to relax tense muscles, promote blood flow, and calm the nervous system, leading to a reduction in back pain symptoms and an improvement in overall comfort.
remedy_properties: Cayenne pepper: Contains capsaicin, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the muscles and joints.
Ginger: Has anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which may help alleviate back pain by reducing muscle spasms, stiffness, and swelling.
Willow bark: Contains salicin, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin, which can help reduce inflammation and relieve back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing joint pain and stiffness associated with back pain.
Seeds: Bell pepper seeds contain fatty acids and antioxidants that help soothe and moisturize the skin, providing relief from back pain-related muscle spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum peppers and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the peppers by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for 3 days.
Grind the dried peppers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the pepper powder with coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler at medium heat.
Stir the mixture constantly until it reaches a temperature of 160°f and sets into a smooth cream within 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herb, also known as Echinacea, contains compounds that target the root cause of back pain, reducing inflammation and swelling in the affected area. As a natural alternative to pharmaceuticals, herbal purple coneflower creams provide effective relief from chronic and acute back pain, promoting relaxation, flexibility, and mobility. By soothing sore muscles and joints, these creams alleviate tension and discomfort, allowing individuals to move freely without restriction.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation in the muscles and joints, which can contribute to back pain.

Pain-relieving properties: The presence of alkaloids like eupatoriopicrin and parthenolide in the cream have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, providing relief from chronic back pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in the cream help to protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to muscle and joint degeneration that may exacerbate back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce back pain and inflammation.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and antioxidants, seeds are used to create topical creams that promote relaxation and relieve muscle tension associated with back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm oven at 150°f for 8 hours or air dry them slowly.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea root into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered echinacea with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 10 minutes stirring occasionally until the wax melts and the mixture reaches 180°f.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with back pain because of its unique combination of calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The soothing aroma of lavender oil can ease muscle tension and relax the body, reducing stiffness and discomfort in the lower back. Additionally, the cream's emollient qualities help to moisturize and nourish the skin, promoting flexibility and relieving pressure on joints. As a result, English lavender creams provide natural relief for those suffering from chronic back pain, helping to improve posture, reduce strain, and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with back pain.
Analgesic: Lavender's analgesic properties can help alleviate pain by blocking the production of pain messengers in the brain, providing relief from chronic back pain.

Muscle relaxant: The sedative and calming effects of lavender oil can help relax tense muscles, reduce muscle spasms, and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial for individuals experiencing back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
Buds: Containing high amounts of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which provide analgesic and calming properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including dried lavandula angustifolia flowers 10 grams, coconut oil 200 milliliters and beeswax 20 grams.
Mix the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with coconut oil in a double boiler at medium heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the herbal solids after cooling to room temperature.
Melt the beeswax in a separate container and slowly add it to the lavandula angustifolia-infused coconut oil, stirring constantly at low heat for 10 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature before use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with back pain because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This ingredient helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the muscles and joints, which are common contributors to back pain. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects can help to soothe and relax tense muscles, reducing stiffness and discomfort. By providing both pain relief and muscle relaxation, herbal licorice creams offer a natural and effective way to alleviate back pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the muscles and joints, alleviating back pain.

Muscle relaxant properties: The herbal extracts present in licorice cream, such as camphor and menthol, have a calming effect on muscle spasms, allowing for increased flexibility and reduced stiffness that can contribute to back pain.

Antioxidant activity: Licorice cream's antioxidant properties help protect the body from free radical damage caused by oxidative stress, which can contribute to chronic low back pain, while also promoting overall spinal health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with back pain.
Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has analgesic and antispasmodic properties, helping to relieve back muscle spasms and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 10 minutes.
Combine 20g of the powdered root with 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat it at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Add 10g of beeswax and stir until melted, then remove from heat and let cool to 40°c for 15 minutes.
Mix 5g of vitamin e oil and 2.5g of lavender essential oil with the cooled mixture and blend until well combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized cream jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, allowing it to set at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with back pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as gallic acid and salicylic acid, work together to reduce swelling and ease tension in the muscles. As a result, damask rose creams can provide quick relief from chronic lower back pain caused by strain or injury. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects help to relax the muscles, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing stiffness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: The gallic acid present in damask roses helps reduce inflammation, which can alleviate back pain caused by conditions such as arthritis or muscle strain.
Pain-relieving property: The salicylic acid content in damask roses provides a natural alternative to NSAIDs (nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs), helping to relieve back pain and stiffness.
Antispasmodic property: Damask rose extracts can relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can help alleviate back pain caused by conditions such as sciatica or muscle cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like phenolic acids and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate back pain.
Seeds: Containing a high amount of fatty acids, particularly omega-3 and omega-6, which provide anti-inflammatory properties to soothe back discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried rosa damascena petals from trusted sources to ensure quality.
Combine the dried petals with 50g of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a heat-proof glass container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes to extract the essential oils.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 50ml of the infused oil and heat at 70°c for 10 minutes to create a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant creams helps with back pain because they possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the muscles. The cream's active ingredients, such as curcumin and ginger, work together to provide a soothing effect on the affected area, allowing for improved mobility and reduced stiffness. This natural remedy can be particularly beneficial for those who suffer from chronic back pain or experience temporary discomfort due to strain or overexertion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Curry leaves contain compounds like curcuminoids and flavonoids that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with back pain.

Pain-relieving properties: The herbaceous oil extracted from curry leaves has been shown to possess analgesic and anesthetic properties, which can help numb the pain and provide relief from back pain.
Muscle relaxant properties: Curry leaves contain compounds that have a relaxing effect on muscles, which can help reduce muscle spasms and tension that often accompany back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the curry plant contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving back pain.
Barks: The barks of the curry plant have been traditionally used to treat musculoskeletal issues such as back pain due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 20g of dried helichrysum italicum flowers, 100g of sweet almond oil, and 50g of beeswax.
Combine the dried helichrysum italicum flowers and 50ml of hot water in a heat-resistant container to create a floral infusion.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the solids.
Mix the strained floral infusion with 100g of sweet almond oil, 50g of beeswax, and 10ml of vitamin e oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low temperature for 30-40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted to create a smooth cream texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark creams helps with back pain because it contains a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called cinchona alkaloids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the muscles and joints. These compounds also have natural analgesic properties, providing relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, Jesuit's bark has been traditionally used to relax tense muscles and improve circulation, further aiding in the reduction of back pain symptoms. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective way to alleviate back pain without resorting to harsh medications or invasive procedures.
remedy_properties: Cinchona alkaloids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help to reduce pain and swelling associated with back pain.

Tannins: The tannins in Jesuit's Bark cream have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the affected area, thereby alleviating back pain.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Jesuit's Bark cream have antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, contributing to relief from back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain high levels of quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory that helps to reduce swelling and ease muscle strain in the back.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona contain cinchonine, a compound that has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective for relieving back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinchona officinalis leaves and dry them properly using a food dehydrator for 24 hours at low heat.
Grind the dried cinchona officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes until smooth.
Combine the powdered cinchona officinalis with 20% witch hazel, 30% coconut oil, and 10% beeswax in a double boiler for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Heat the mixture to 180°f to melt the beeswax and mix well for an additional 10 minutes until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for at least 2 weeks before use to allow the ingredients to mature and blend together effectively.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with back pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and relax tense muscles. The cream's active ingredient, angelic acid, works to reduce muscle spasms and stiffness, providing rapid relief from chronic back pain. Additionally, the cream's ability to increase blood flow to the affected area promotes healing and reduces swelling, further alleviating discomfort and promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. As a result, many people have found significant relief from back pain by using angelica creams as part of their treatment regimen.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Angelica creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as angelic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the muscles and joints, providing relief from back pain.
Muscle relaxants: Angelica contains muscle-relaxing compounds like estragole, which can help to relax tense muscles and reduce spasms, easing discomfort and stiffness associated with back pain.

Analgesic properties: Angelica creams also contain analgesic compounds like isoalantolactone, which helps to numb the affected area, reducing pain perception and providing relief from acute or chronic back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are used to create creams that target inflammation and reduce pain associated with back issues.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome's high concentration of angelic acid and isoangelic acid makes it an effective ingredient in creams for relieving muscle spasms and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried angelica archangelica root and stem, carefully selecting fresh and organic material.
Combine the harvested herb with 30 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar, stirring until well combined.
Place the mixture in a warm oven at 40 degrees celsius for 12 hours to infuse, allowing the herbal compounds to extract.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of the herbal infused oil in a double boiler, stirring until melted and well combined to create the final cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with back pain because it contains methyl salicylate, a natural analgesic that targets sore muscles and joints. When applied topically to affected areas, the cream reduces inflammation and relieves tension, providing fast-acting relief from acute or chronic back pain. The warm, soothing sensation also helps to relax the muscles, reducing stiffness and improving range of motion. As the cream absorbs into the skin, it continues to provide a gentle, yet effective, relief from discomfort, making wintergreen creams an excellent natural remedy for back pain sufferers.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a key component of wintergreen oil, helps to relieve muscle and joint pain by numbing the skin and reducing inflammation, providing rapid relief from back pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wintergreen oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as methyl salicylate, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the muscles and joints, which can contribute to back pain caused by conditions like arthritis or sprains.

Analgesic effects: The analgesic properties of wintergreen oil, including its ability to block pain pathways and reduce pain transmission, help provide effective pain relief for back pain sufferers without causing addiction or other side effects.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing high levels of salicylic acid, the roots are used to create a natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving agent in creams for back pain.
Rhizomes: As the thickened underground stems of the plant, rhizomes provide a concentrated source of wintergreen's medicinal compounds, making them ideal for cream production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in the early morning after dew has dried off completely.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place with good air circulation at room temperature for 7 days.
Steep 10 grams of dried gaultheria procumbens in 500 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil for 2 hours under gentle heat.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids and setting aside for later use.
Mix 30 percent of the infused gaultheria procumbens oil with beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler at low heat until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with back pain because of its unique anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The gel-like substance present in aloe vera has been shown to reduce swelling and relieve discomfort associated with back pain. Additionally, its cooling and soothing effects help to relax tense muscles and calm irritated tissues, providing quick relief from acute and chronic back pain episodes. This natural remedy is particularly effective for alleviating lower back pain, sciatica, and strains, making it an excellent alternative to traditional pain management methods.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal aloe creams contain compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with back pain.
Analgesic properties: Aloe vera's analgesic properties help to numb the area affected by back pain, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Wound healing and tissue regeneration properties: The antioxidants and vitamins present in aloe vera creams promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can be beneficial for treating chronic back pain conditions that involve skin irritation or wounds.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel inside the leaves is rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making it an effective ingredient for soothing and reducing back pain.
Roots: The roots of the aloe plant contain anthraquinones, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves using scissors or a sharp knife.
Extract 3 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and mix it with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil.
Add 1 teaspoon of turmeric powder and 1/2 teaspoon of ginger powder to the mixture.
Stir in 10 drops of lavender essential oil and 5 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized glass jar, store it at room temperature for 24 hours before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with back pain because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that effectively soothe and calm irritated muscles and joints. The cream's active ingredients, such as calendula extract, penetrate deep into the skin to reduce swelling and ease stiffness, providing quick relief from acute back pain. Additionally, pot marigold's natural antioxidant properties help to promote healing and reduce scar tissue formation, leading to long-term pain reduction and improved flexibility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with back pain, allowing for improved mobility and reduced pain.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds in pot marigold cream work to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and tissue damage, promoting healing and reducing chronic pain.
Analgesic: Pot marigold cream's analgesic properties help numb the painful area, providing relief from acute back pain and discomfort, making it easier to move and perform daily activities.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and relieve back pain by reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds have analgesic properties that help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation, making them an effective ingredient in topical creams for back pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried calendula flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Mix the ground calendula powder with 50ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 20ml of beeswax in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add 10 drops of lavender essential oil and stir well to combine, then heat the mixture at 60°c for 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before whipping it with an electric mixer until it reaches a smooth consistency within 5 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at 4°c for up to 6 months, allowing the ingredients to mature for optimal efficacy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red shiso creams helps with back pain because of its unique blend of herbs, which work together to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate pressure on the spine and surrounding tissues, while its analgesic components numb the pain, providing instant relief. Additionally, red shiso's natural moisturizing effects soothe dry skin and promote healthy joint function, further contributing to a reduction in back discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Red shiso's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as shisonenol, help reduce swelling and relieve pain associated with back pain by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Muscle relaxant: The camphor content in red shiso has a relaxing effect on muscles, which can help alleviate muscle spasms and tension that often contribute to back pain.
Pain-relieving: Red shiso's analgesic properties, due to the presence of cinnamic acid, help block pain signals from reaching the brain, providing natural relief for back pain sufferers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Rhyzomes: Contain antioxidants and flavonoids that soothe and calm irritated muscles and joints, reducing discomfort caused by back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lithospermum erythrorhizon roots in late summer weighing approximately 250 grams.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dry place for 7-10 days or using a food dehydrator at 55°c.
Grind dried lithospermum erythrorhizon root powder to fine consistency using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of ground lithospermum erythrorhizon powder with 80 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes at 60°c while stirring occasionally to create a smooth and creamy consistency suitable for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with back pain because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that work to reduce muscle soreness and stiffness. The warming properties of ginger also help to relax tight muscles, reducing tension and discomfort in the back. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of creams can help to soothe dry skin and reduce irritation, allowing for improved circulation and reduced pain. This combination of anti-inflammatory and soothing properties makes herbal ginger creams a natural and effective remedy for back pain relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal ginger creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help reduce inflammation in the muscles and joints, alleviating back pain caused by arthritis, muscle strains, or other inflammatory conditions.

Analgesic and anesthetic properties: The natural oils and extracts present in herbal ginger creams have analgesic (pain-relieving) and anesthetic properties, helping to numb the affected area, reducing discomfort, and providing relief from chronic back pain.

Muscle relaxant compounds: Herbal ginger creams contain muscle relaxant compounds like camphor and menthol, which help soothe tense muscles, reduce spasms, and promote relaxation, thereby alleviating back pain caused by muscle strain or tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in natural compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Rhizomes: The main storage organ of the ginger plant, rhizomes contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, making them effective in relieving back pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh turmeric rhizomes and dried ginger root and grind them into fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Melt one cup of coconut oil in a double boiler over medium heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally until clear.
Combine the ground turmeric and ginger powder with three tablespoons of beeswax pellets in a small bowl and mix well for 5 minutes.
Gradually add the turmeric-ginger mixture to the melted coconut oil whisking continuously for 2 minutes until smooth.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for at least two hours before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saffron creams helps with back pain because they contain a unique combination of anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that target the affected areas, providing relief from discomfort and stiffness. The natural compounds in saffron cream work to reduce swelling and inflammation in the muscles and joints, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility. Additionally, saffron's potent antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals, promoting a healthy environment for optimal healing and recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Saffron's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as crocin and crocetin, help reduce inflammation in the muscles and joints, which can contribute to back pain.
Pain-relieving properties: Saffron contains analgesic compounds that can help relieve back pain by reducing nerve impulses and blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals.

Muscle relaxant properties: The flavonoids and other bioactive compounds in saffron creams may help relax tense muscles, reduce muscle spasms, and improve flexibility, all of which can alleviate back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate back pain.
Barks: Containing antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and relax muscles affected by back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried crocus sativus flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered crocus sativus with 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture for 30 minutes to create an infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the oil extract.
Combine the oil extract with 50 grams of beeswax and heat it gently in a double boiler until the wax has fully melted and incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes before filling sterilized tubes or jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree peony creams helps with back pain because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that work in harmony to reduce inflammation and alleviate muscle tension. The anti-inflammatory properties of tree peony help to soothe sore muscles and joints, while the soothing agents calm irritated nerves and promote relaxation. This natural approach can provide long-lasting relief from chronic back pain, allowing individuals to move with greater ease and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal tree peony creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate back pain by decreasing swelling and discomfort.

Analgesic and anesthetic effects: The creams' analgesic properties help numb the affected area, reducing pain perception, while their anesthetic effects can block the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing fast relief from back pain.
Muscle relaxant properties: Herbal tree peony creams contain compounds that relax muscles, which helps reduce muscle spasms and strain in the back, leading to improved mobility and reduced pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing bioactive compounds, tree peony roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate back pain.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, rhyzomes provide potent analgesic effects in treating back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest paeonia suffruticosa roots in the fall when mature, dry them thoroughly and grind into fine powder.
Combine 250 grams of ground paeonia suffruticosa roots with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Infuse the mixture for at least 4 hours or overnight by placing it in a double boiler or a dark glass jar and letting it sit at room temperature.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, squeezing out as much oil as possible from the solids.

Mix the resulting herbal oil with 250 grams of beeswax, 100 milliliters of vitamin e oil and 50 milliliters of distilled water to create the finished cream, allowing it to solidify at room temperature before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with back pain because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and relax tense muscles, reducing discomfort and stiffness. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and taraxadiol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area. Additionally, dandelion creams can help to improve blood flow and reduce muscle spasms, making them a natural and effective solution for alleviating back pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the back.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which is often associated with chronic back pain conditions like arthritis or fibromyalgia.
Natural analgesic: Dandelion contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has natural analgesic and anti-pain properties that can help soothe back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Fruits: Containing flavonoids, fatty acids, and terpenes, the fruits of dandelion plant help to relax muscles, reduce spasms, and ease back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in bulk quantities of 100g.
Combine the harvested ingredients with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for a ratio of 20%.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c to 70°c for a period of 1 hour to ensure proper infusion.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 10g of beeswax and heat until melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and seal tightly after preparation time has elapsed.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for back pain are topical preparations that combine natural ingredients, such as herbs, essential oils, and botanicals, to provide relief from back ache. These creams work by penetrating deep into the skin to target the affected areas, providing anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce muscle spasms, stiffness, and pain. Examples of herbal creams for back pain include CBD oil-infused creams, arnica gel, and capsaicin cream. By using these creams, individuals can experience reduced inflammation, improved mobility, and a significant decrease in chronic back pain, ultimately enhancing their daily activities and quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat back pain with herbal creams is by using creams containing anti-inflammatory herbs such as arnica, ginger, or willow bark. Apply the cream directly to the affected area for localized relief. Topical application of these herbal ingredients has been shown to reduce inflammation and ease muscle tension, providing effective pain management without harsh chemicals or medications.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to back pain that are treated with herbal creams are various musculoskeletal issues, such as arthritis, fibromyalgia, and sciatica. Herbal creams containing ingredients like capsaicin, arnica, and menthol can provide relief from joint and muscle pain, reducing inflammation and discomfort. These creams can also help alleviate symptoms of conditions like tendonitis, bursitis, and strain injuries, making them a natural alternative to conventional treatments for many painful conditions.
